title: WQO — Operational Problem Entry
description: Schema for enterprise knowledge/documentation operational problem entries. Each entry maps a real-world failure mode to WQO text-quality concerns and project tooling.
x-note: system_boundary is the bridging dimension between WQO text concerns (how text fails) and operational problems (where in the doc lifecycle failure occurs).

title.description: Short display name of the problem.

problem_statement.description: One to three sentences describing what fails and the observable symptom.

causal_mechanism.description: The root cause or trigger — why this failure occurs. Often a process gap, architectural assumption, or missing gate.

domain_category.description: High-level domain category grouping related problems.

system_boundary.description: The stage in the documentation lifecycle where this failure manifests. Bridges WQO text-quality concerns and operational failure modes. authoring=while writing; versioning=at VCS/commit time; build-publish=SSG/CI/CD/migration; consumption=at read time by humans or machines; governance=ownership/review/policy layer; infrastructure=hardware/platform constraints.

impact.description: Downstream real-world consequences when this problem is unresolved. Reuses WQO D10 impact vocabulary.

risk_severity.description: Stakes of failure. Reuses WQO D11 vocabulary. cosmetic=low; functional=blocks tasks; credibility=erodes trust; safety=security/compliance/production risk.

resolution_pattern.description: Short name for the fix archetype. Examples: ci-gate, ownership-protocol, content-audit, genre-enforcement, semantic-linting, metadata-expiry, audit-gate, model-sizing, ux-design, prompt-chain.

wqo_links.description: IDs of WQO concept nodes (from concerns/*.md or concept.schema.json) that this problem is addressed by or directly related to.

project_links.description: Projects that detect, address, generate, consume, or govern this problem.

path.description: Absolute filesystem path to the project root.

relationship.description: detects=surfaces the problem; addresses=fixes or mitigates it; generates=produces content that may manifest the problem; consumes=ingests affected content; governs=owns policy over the problem domain.
